title: MAX6675 K-Type Thermocouple SPI Module
design_question: How to measure high temperatures (0–1000 °C) with a K-type thermocouple and deliver a digital reading to an Arduino via SPI?
selected_approach: MAX6675 breakout module with K-type thermocouple probe (SS316 sheath, 1 m lead). 3-wire SPI (CS, CLK, MISO); CS on D10. Adafruit MAX6675 library. Read every 250 ms minimum. Returns temperature in 0.25 °C steps. Companion probe: ungrounded junction probe avoids electrical interference from heater elements.
rationale: MAX6675 provides integrated cold-junction compensation eliminating a separate reference thermistor. 12-bit (0.25 °C) resolution is adequate for reflow oven and barbecue monitor applications. Breakout modules cost < £3 and include decoupling capacitors. Ungrounded probe prevents ground-loop issues when heater element shares system ground.

alternatives.reason_rejected: MAX31855 is the newer successor with 14-bit resolution and fault diagnostics but costs 2× more; MAX6675 adequate for 0–1000 °C applications.
NTC maximum operating temperature typically 125 °C — cannot be used for reflow oven (target 250 °C) or forge applications.

verification_notes: FACT: 'MAX6675 K-Type Thermocouple-to-Digital SPI Converter' confirms 0–1024 °C range, 0.25 °C resolution, and 250 ms minimum read interval.
